steps.title: Recognize vertex form from the given maximum
steps.content:
The seal reaches maximum depth at $t = 6$ minutes with depth 302.4 meters. This vertex point $(6, 302.4)$ directly gives us vertex form:

$$g(t) = a(t - 6)^2 + 302.4$$

We don't need to expand or solve a system—the vertex is already built into the equation.

steps.title: Find coefficient a using the surface point
steps.content:
The seal reaches the surface (depth = 0) at $t = 12$ minutes. Substitute into the vertex form:

$$0 = a(12 - 6)^2 + 302.4$$
$$0 = 36a + 302.4$$
$$a = -\frac{302.4}{36} = -8.4$$

So the complete function is $g(t) = -8.4(t - 6)^2 + 302.4$.

steps.title: Evaluate at t = 10 minutes
steps.content:
Substitute $t = 10$ into the function:

$$g(10) = -8.4(10 - 6)^2 + 302.4$$
$$g(10) = -8.4(16) + 302.4$$
$$g(10) = -134.4 + 302.4$$
$$g(10) = 168$$

The estimated depth of the seal 10 minutes after entering the water was 168 meters.
